title: carya laciniosa
intro: Carya laciniosa, also known as the shellbark hickory, is a deciduous tree species native to eastern North America. The bark of this tree has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The shellbark hickory has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, thanks to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The bark can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
